focus: Ground-truth the current Copilot CLI 1.0.34 hook-config schema from primary sources

graphEvents.summary: Official docs still use flat bash/powershell command fields
Live copilot -p debug blocked by SecItemCopyMatching -50 in sandbox

summary: Current GitHub docs still document hook command objects with flat type/bash/powershell/cwd/timeoutSec fields; no primary-source evidence found for command/run/shell replacement schema.
Copilot CLI hook discovery is repository .github/hooks/*.json from current working directory; ~/.copilot/config.json is not the hook source in the inspected run.

The current root superset-notify.json is schema-shaped correctly but calls only the Superset notification hook; it no longer invokes system-spec-kit session-start or user-prompt-submitted writer scripts.
The active Superset wrapper rewrites .github/hooks/superset-notify.json whenever SUPERSET_TAB_ID is set, so patching only the repo JSON is not durable.
The missing bash/powershell error appears in logs across multiple older Copilot CLI versions, weakening the hypothesis that it is a same-day 1.0.34 schema regression.
COPILOT_DEBUG=1 copilot -p did not reach hook parsing under Codex sandbox because the Homebrew binary failed with SecItemCopyMatching -50.
NVM npm packages @github/copilot 0.0.334 and 0.0.354 are not the active 1.0.34 Homebrew binary, and direct package scans found no hooks.schema.json or exact error string.

Replace direct Superset hook commands with docs-aligned script entries using cwd .github/hooks: sessionStart -> ./scripts/session-start.sh, userPromptSubmitted -> ./scripts/user-prompt-submitted.sh, sessionEnd/postToolUse/preToolUse -> ./scripts/superset-notify.sh EVENT. Also patch the Superset wrapper template or the JSON will be overwritten.
No evidence found that flat bash was replaced by command/run/shell in current GitHub docs; current docs still show bash/powershell.
Run the proposed patched config from an authenticated terminal, stop wrapper clobbering, and force Copilot to reveal which hook object lacks bash/powershell.
